name: University Of South Alabama
description_short: University of South Alabama Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving the university community with banking and financial services from its main office in Mobile, AL.
description_long: University of South Alabama Federal Credit Union is a federal credit union serving members affiliated with the University of South Alabama. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, it operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The credit union model emphasizes member benefits and community focus rather than shareholder profits. The institution offers standard credit union financial services typical of member-owned cooperatives, including share accounts, share certificates, and other savings and lending products. As a federal credit union, it is regulated by the NCUA and must maintain specific member eligibility requirements, typically tied to employment, location, or association with the University of South Alabama. The credit union operates its main office in Mobile, Alabama at 103 Hillcrest Road, with phone support available at (251) 706-0255. The institution maintains multiple branches, allowing members access to services across different locations. As a smaller, mission-focused credit union, it likely emphasizes personalized service and member advocacy. Prospective members should verify eligibility requirements before joining, as federal credit unions restrict membership to specific groups.
pros: Federal credit union status means NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure prioritizes member benefits over shareholder returns
Multiple branch locations provide access beyond the main Mobile office
Direct phone support available at main office for member inquiries
Affiliated with University of South Alabama, providing community focus and local expertise
Not-for-profit status typically results in lower fees and competitive rates compared to commercial banks
cons: Membership eligibility is restricted—likely limited to University of South Alabama affiliates
Limited service details available online; requires direct contact for product information
Smaller institution may have fewer ATM locations and digital services compared to major banks
No information provided about specific loan products, rates, or terms
Geographic limitation to Mobile, AL area may inconvenience non-local members
best_for: University of South Alabama employees and students seeking member-focused banking
Members who value not-for-profit financial institutions and community banking principles
Individuals in the Mobile, Alabama area looking for local credit union services
diagnosis: University of South Alabama Federal Credit Union is best for University of South Alabama employees and students who meet membership eligibility requirements and seek member-owned, not-for-profit banking with local service. The primary caveat is that specific product offerings, rates, and eligibility criteria require direct contact with the institution, as detailed service information is not available online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
